Step 3: UpdateTrip(title='Updated Title Only' only) + GetTrip — title updated, status=TRIP_STATUS_ACTIVE PRESERVED (not reset to PLANNING), description PRESERVED.
Step 4: UpdateTrip(status=TRIP_STATUS_COMPLETED only) + GetTrip — status changed to COMPLETED, title='Updated Title Only' PRESERVED, description PRESERVED.

Step 5: ListTrips — found trip with final state: title='Updated Title Only', description='This trip tests that partial updates do not wipe unrelated fields.', status=TRIP_STATUS_COMPLETED. Matches all Step 4 assertions.

bugs.title: testctl generates JWT tokens missing iss/aud claims
bugs.description: The user-provided token (generated by an older testctl binary) was rejected with 'token is missing required claim: aud claim is required, token is missing required claim: iss claim is required'. The testctl source code at cmd/testctl/main.go:118-119 includes iss/aud claims, but stale compiled binaries (e.g. from 'go run' cache or previous 'go install') may lack them. Rebuilding with 'go build ./cmd/testctl' produces valid tokens. This is an operational issue — any developer running an outdated testctl binary will get auth failures with no obvious cause.

usefulness_evaluation.notes: Structural regression test — no AI involved. All scores 0 (not applicable).

feature_coverage: UpdateTrip (partial update — status only, PLANNING -> ACTIVE)
UpdateTrip (partial update — title only, status preserved)
UpdateTrip (partial update — status only, ACTIVE -> COMPLETED)
COALESCE partial update preservation (3 distinct partial updates verified, zero field wipes)
summary: All 5 steps passed. COALESCE partial updates work correctly: updating status alone preserves title and description, updating title alone preserves status and description, and a second status-only update preserves the previously-updated title. No field wipe regressions detected. Status transitions PLANNING -> ACTIVE -> COMPLETED all succeeded. Note: the user-provided JWT token was rejected due to missing iss/aud claims (stale testctl binary); a fresh token was generated via rebuilt testctl.
